home.title: BE A PART OF SOMETHING MARVELLOUS
home.body: OmniBot is a modular Discord bot with a protected Activity panel, Creator Alerts, Dev Blog publishing, dynamic voice rooms, analytics and a local AI Moderator connected to the current production stack.
home.feature.ai.title: Moderation With AI Signals
home.feature.ai.text: Classic moderation commands, logs, punishment history and local AI Moderator checks help staff react faster while keeping humans in control.
home.feature.creator.title: Creator & Developer Tools
home.feature.creator.text: Stream alerts, video notifications, Dev Blog publishing and embed builders help creators and developers share updates beautifully.
home.feature.activity.title: Activity Control Surface
home.feature.activity.text: Dashboard, bot settings, integrations, health checks, statistics and access rules live in one focused Discord Activity.
home.feature.voice.title: Dynamic Voice Rooms
home.feature.voice.text: Temporary voice channels with owner controls for naming, user limits, lock states and ownership transfer, with cleanup when rooms go empty.
home.feature.stats.title: Server Statistics & Insights
home.feature.stats.text: Track message activity, voice time, top members, channel trends and member movement in one clear dashboard.
home.feature.logs.title: Logging & Audit Trail
home.feature.logs.text: Message edits, deletions, role changes and moderation actions are logged with configurable retention for transparent admin history.
home.ai.heading: Local moderation intelligence, controlled by your server rules.
home.ai.body: OmniBot does not treat AI as a magic ban button. Selected Discord channels are sent to a local AI Moderator API, where rules, semantic checks, ruBERT classification, risk scoring and policy resolution work together. The result is a clear moderation signal that admins can tune from Activity.
home.ai.signal.title: Signals before punishment
home.ai.signal.text: AI Moderator returns labels, risk score, confidence, reasons and recommended actions. Staff policy still defines what can happen next.
home.ai.model.title: Local ruBERT model
home.ai.model.text: The moderation API can load the trained ruBERT tiny2 model locally and use CUDA on the server when NVIDIA drivers are available.
home.ai.control.title: Activity controlled
home.ai.control.text: Admins choose covered channels, blacklist words, allowed domains, label thresholds and maximum action levels from the Activity panel.
home.ai.audit.title: Audit friendly
home.ai.audit.text: Health checks, policy decisions and moderation metadata stay visible for troubleshooting, review and safer iteration.
home.stack.heading: One Activity. Many workspaces. Local AI checks.
home.stack.body: Every module, from roles and logs to stream alerts, Dev Blog posts and AI moderation, lives in one dashboard instead of scattered commands. Admins see what changed, who has access, which services are healthy and what needs review without digging through channel history.
home.stack.creator: Creator Alerts
about.title: OMNIBOT IS A CONTROL LAYER FOR COMMUNITIES
about.intro: OmniBot began as a self-hosted alternative to paid, fragmented Discord bots. It is built for growing communities that need automation, creator publishing, local AI moderation and clear control over data, roles and server decisions.
about.who.title: A practical toolkit for communities that outgrow command-only bots.
about.who.text: OmniBot combines moderation commands, local AI checks, automation, creator tools and admin workflows in one Discord Activity. The public layer explains the product, while the panel gives each role a focused workspace.
about.architecture.title: Frontend -> Backend API -> Discord, DB and bot services.
about.architecture.text: The browser never stores the bot token and never reaches Discord APIs directly. OAuth, permission checks and persistence stay behind the backend boundary.
about.principles.label: What we stand for
about.principle.control.title: Control
about.principle.control.text: Admins keep the final say over roles, alerts, logs and publishing. The Activity makes decisions visible before they become Discord actions.
about.principle.automation.title: Automation
about.principle.automation.text: Repeated setup work moves into clean workflows instead of long command chains. Welcome messages, role panels and voice rooms can be maintained without memorizing syntax.
about.principle.safety.title: Safety
about.principle.safety.text: Sensitive actions pass through backend permission checks. Local AI moderation produces review signals and policy decisions without becoming a replacement for server staff.
about.principle.creativity.title: Creativity
about.principle.creativity.text: Creators and developers get focused spaces for alerts, Dev Blog updates and previews. The goal is to publish better updates with less operational noise.
about.pillars.title: Four areas, one control surface.
about.pillar.ai: AI Moderation: local ruBERT-backed classification, rules, review signals and policy-controlled actions.
about.pillar.community: Community Management: welcome flows, role panels, logs and clear access rules.
about.pillar.creator: Creator Ecosystem: Twitch, YouTube and Kick alerts shaped for stream communities.
about.pillar.developer: Developer Publishing: Dev Blog drafts, previews and controlled publishing paths.
about.fact.modules: core modules from moderation to voice rooms
about.fact.roles: role workspaces for users, creators, developers and admins
about.fact.activity: Activity surface instead of scattered command setup
about.voice.title: Dynamic Voice Rooms
about.voice.text: Temporary voice spaces stay tidy with owner controls, limits, lock states and cleanup when a room is empty.
about.voice.label: Voice
about.stats.title: Server Statistics
about.stats.text: Message trends, voice time and member movement become readable without exporting logs or scanning channels.
about.stats.label: Insights
about.logs.title: Logging
about.logs.text: Edits, deletions, role changes and moderation actions land in an audit trail with retention controls.
about.logs.label: Audit
about.roles.title: Access follows the person opening the Activity.
about.roles.standard: Standard users see public information and allowed self-service modules.
about.roles.creator: Creators can manage alert sources connected to their publishing workflow.
about.roles.developer: Developers can prepare Dev Blog updates and technical announcements.
about.roles.admin: Admins get the full control panel for configuration and audit work.
about.map.title: Built to grow without adding clutter.
about.map.text: Each module can become its own workspace while sharing the same navigation, permissions, loading states and API client.
